mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0%, labor market slack suppresses corporate profit margins and consumer spending, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 7.0%, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment remains above 7.0% for more than 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals synchronized consumer demand growth, which drives corporate revenue acceleration and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive return differential compared to baseline when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism reverses direction during late-cycle expansions, producing negative returns when INDPRO rises, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 50% in any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening labor demand and rising household income, which directly supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth consistently fails to predict positive 5-day forward returns, or the correlation coefficient remains below 0.05 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 230.0, inflationary pressures trigger expectations of monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL surpasses 230.0, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions at a 95% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic demand, which accelerates corporate earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models, or forward returns turn negative when both series expand simultaneously in > 40% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, rising industrial production signals economic overheating and impending monetary tightening, which triggers risk-off flows and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive returns during late-cycle phases, or the regime classification fails to separate return distributions, resulting in a negative Sharpe ratio for the strategy.
